articles.headline: Harry Kane vs. Erling Haaland: How do England, Norway stars compare? Key stats before World Cup clash
articles.description: Who will come out on top in the World Cup quarterfinal, Harry Kane or Erling Haaland? Here is what the stats say.

images.name: Harry Kane Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.alt: An image of Harry Kane alongside one of Erling Haaland.
images.credit: Photo by Luis Cano/Jam Media/Getty Images and Photo by Richard Sellers/Sportsphoto/Allstar via Getty Images

images.name: Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.alt: Erling Haaland wears a viking helmet.
images.credit: (Photo by Julian Finney - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images)

images.name: Harry Kane penalty [1296x729]
images.alt: Harry Kane takes the penalty against Mexico.

images.name: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'
images.caption: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'

articles.headline: Police officer injured, arrests made amid London disorder after France beat Morocco
articles.description: A police officer was injured and four people were arrested after disorder broke out in London following France's 2-0 win against Morocco in the World Cup quarterfinal on Thursday.

images.name: Metropolitan Police stock [1296x729]
images.alt: The logo of the Metropolitan Police can be seen on a police car in central London.
images.credit: Anna Ross/picture alliance via Getty Images

articles.headline: Instead of its biggest World Cup star, Pulisic was U.S.'s biggest mystery
articles.description: In his prime at a World Cup on home soil, this summer was Christian Pulisic's moment, but for a number of reasons, he failed to meet it.

articles.headline: World Cup quarterfinals: How we got here, predictions, odds
articles.description: Four weeks after the World Cup began, the quarterfinals are underway, with each team three wins from lifting the trophy. Next up, Spain vs. Belgium.

images.name: Burley: Spain need more than Yamal to win World Cup
images.caption: Burley: Spain need more than Yamal to win World Cup

article.headline: Stephen Eustáquio stoppage-time heroics send Canada to World Cup last-16
article.description: Stephen Eustáquio scored in second-half stoppage time, and Canada beat South Africa 1-0 to earn their first knockout match victory in a World Cup.
article.linkText: Eustáquio heroics send Canada to WCup last-16

article.story:
Stephen Eustáquio scored in the second minute of second-half stoppage time, and Canada beat South Africa 1-0 on Sunday for their first knockout match victory in a World Cup.

A tense match at SoFi Stadium appeared to be headed for extra time until Eustáquio -- who plays professionally for Los Angeles FC several miles away -- put a stunning volley from outside the penalty area into the bottom corner of Ronwen Williams' net.

Co-host Canada hung on with strong defensive play in the final minutes and advanced to face the Netherlands or Morocco in Houston on Saturday, July 4.

Coach Jesse Marsch gathered his players in a huddle after the whistle and gave a spirited speech, declaring: "You guys are Canadian heroes! Canadian heroes!"

Canada played their first three matches at home in Toronto and Vancouver, but were forced to become the first World Cup host to play on the road when their lost 2-1 to Switzerland last Wednesday.

The Canadians were still the clear crowd favorite in the Los Angeles area between their traveling fans and thousands of supportive locals, including several in Eustáquio's LAFC shirt who couldn't have imagined the day they were about to enjoy.

Williams made five saves for South Africa, which managed to hang in until the final moments in search of another historic win for the team known as Bafana Bafana. Canada had the best chances all day, but underdog South Africa stayed firm defensively and created a handful of excellent chances itself.

Canada finally capitalized when Alistair Johnston's long pass into the box was cleared directly into the path of Eustáquio, who struck it purely and started to celebrate almost immediately.

After finishing second in their respective groups, both South Africa and Canada were playing for their nations' first knockout victories at a World Cup when they opened the round of 32 with the only match of the day.

Canada got a boost in the 75th minute when star defender Alphonso Davies came on for his first World Cup action after missing group play with a hamstring injury.

The playmaking Bayern Munich defender missed group play after injuring his hamstring last month for the third time this year, but finally returned in the same stadium where he tore a knee ligament in March 2025 in CONCACAF Nations League play.

Davies immediately made the best pass of the day to set up Promise David for a golden chance, but he hit it wide.

South Africa reached the knockout rounds for the first time with a stunning 1-0 victory over South Korea last Wednesday, arguably the highest moment in Bafana Bafana's checkered international history.

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)
